learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of the basic principles and issues that arise in mining and resources law in Australia and internationally.
Analyse and interpret fact patterns to identify legal risk to resources projects at the exploration, project development and operational stages.
Develop a detailed understanding of mining and resources joint venture agreements how they affect the life cycle of a project.
Continue to develop a critical appraisal of the place of equity, contract and property law concepts and issues in the mining and resources sector.

Demonstrate an understanding of key themes shaping the evolving regulatory framework for the mining sector including climate change, resource nationalism, infrastructure access, shipping and transport concerns, and overlapping tenure rights among mining, agricultural and petroleum stakeholders.

assessments.task_description:
On Saturday 29 August during the morning’s lectures there will be a 20 question multiple choice test/exam. The material to be covered in the multiple choice questions will only relate to the previous weekend’s lectures given on 22 and 23 August 2026.

 

Each question will be worth 2 marks.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
An exam period exam will be set on a date to be advised by the central examination centre. This end of semester exam (early November), worth 60 marks, will involve the following:

 

On the last afternoon of lectures on 30 August 2026, we will provide 10 cases already referred to in the course lectures. There will then be September and October for you to read and study them in detail.

 

The exam will involve questions on 3 of the cases. You will not know until you get into the exam which cases will be the subject of those questions. You will therefore need to read and review all the 10 cases to make sure that you can answer questions on 3 of the cases.

 

Each of the three essays will be worth 20 marks, making up the 60 marks in total. The exam will be two hours long. It will be open book. You will therefore have roughly 40 mins for each essay.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

learning_activities.topic:
Day 1

1. Introductions, expectations, grading

2. Enlightenment and post-Enlightenment views of nature

3. Discussion of Natural Resources and Energy Regulation in Australia and Participants Submissions

4. Overview of the Resources Industry in Australia, Canada and internationally

5. Challenges to the Resources Industry: Climate Change and Indigenous Rights

6. Detailed discussion on the Mineral Resources Act and the Common Resources Act structure


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L05

learning_activities.topic:
Day 2

5. Sustainability, closure and post-mining land use
